signals.reasoning: Hold the live LIT short. It is still at peak unrealized PnL with no giveback, the chart remains in a clean lower-high / lower-close sequence, and the mechanical board still shows fresh shorts pressing rather than a completed flush. Main risk is execution discipline because there is no stop set, so manage it tightly rather than treating it as a blind swing.

BTC remains in a fragile post-breakdown state under an active geopolitical veto, but the current move already has long-liquidation characteristics, so this is not a clean fresh position around the live LIT management task.

ETH is weak and still below the recent highs, but after the broader liquidation move this reads more like an already-broken tape than a clean new action point. Keep focus on the live LIT position instead of forcing a second trade.
SOL remains structurally soft, yet the latest move is already extended and the veto brake is active. It can stay on the bearish watchlist, but it is not cleaner than the existing LIT short.
FARTCOIN is still weak, but the price/OI profile is already a long-liquidation flush after a large drop. That makes it lower quality than LIT for active management and not a strong fresh add.
TNSR keeps a bearish drift, but the market depth is too poor to compete with the live LIT short and the current move has already unfolded enough that forcing action here adds noise, not edge.

IP has some squeeze fuel from negative funding, but the tape is still weak and the open-interest crowding is high enough to make this a messy secondary idea rather than something better than the current live position.
NOT is already damaged and thin, with a long-liquidation profile rather than a clean active continuation setup. That puts it firmly in watch-only territory under the current veto regime.
ACE remains bearish on paper, but liquidity is too light and the move is already extended. It is not strong enough to justify attention away from managing the profitable LIT short.
MERL is leaning lower, but the current action still looks like a flush more than a fresh continuation trigger. Under veto, this is a watchlist short rather than an active management priority.
GOAT has extreme squeeze fuel after a violent washout, which cuts against forcing a fresh short here. It is too unstable and too illiquid to be preferable to simply holding and managing LIT.
